records.guidance: active liver disease or unexplained persistent transaminase elevation
active liver disease or unexplained persistent transaminase elevation
not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (coagulopathy/bleeding risk)
contraindicated in hepatic disease with coagulopathy and clinically relevant bleeding risk (Child-Pugh B and C)
contraindicated if liver enzymes greater than 2x ULN or hepatic impairment expected to affect survival
hepatotoxic; avoid in significant liver disease
active hepatic dysfunction; serious hepatotoxicity risk
hepatotoxic; caution in pre-existing liver disease
caution in severe hepatic impairment and chronic alcohol use; hepatotoxicity risk (no dose asserted here)
oral ketoconazole hepatotoxicity; contraindicated in acute or chronic liver disease
active liver disease or ALT greater than 2.5x ULN
risk of cholestatic hepatitis and hepatocellular injury
avoid in significant hepatic impairment (fluid retention, GI bleeding, hepatorenal risk)
